mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly boosting S&P 500 valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous contraction in industrial production and a surge in consumer credit balances indicates a supply-demand mismatch where demand shifts from goods to services, historically preceding equity drawdowns as corporate margins compress.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances where INDPRO falls and RSAFS rises concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-rate environment, housing starts act as a leading indicator of consumer confidence and construction employment; a sustained decline in housing permits combined with elevated policy rates accelerates discount rate repricing, depressing equity valuations.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS level fails to produce negative 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reinforces corporate revenue expectations and reduces recession probability, supporting positive equity forward returns over a one-week horizon as earnings revisions turn upward.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 5-day forward returns in payroll growth months are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPI index exceeds 310, it signals entrenched inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in, leading to multiple compression and negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the CPI threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where nonfarm payrolls grow while housing starts decline indicates a structural shift in consumer spending away from durable goods, which historically precedes margin compression and negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in more than 50% of historical instances where PAYEMS rises and HOUST falls concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth, strong employment gains amplify corporate earnings momentum, driving positive equity returns over a weekly horizon as earnings revisions accelerate ahead of quarterly reports.
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 growth and PAYEMS gains fail to produce positive 5-day forward returns in more than 50% of observed instances.
